macro_views: Small caps entered 2026 with significant momentum and continued to lead through the quarter. The
broader macro backdrop is supportive: a strong labor market, resilient consumer spending, and early signs
of a broadening economic expansion beyond the narrow set of mega-cap technology names that
dominated the last few years.

letters.lead_summary: Admiral Capital is a fundamental, long biased equities investment firm in Charlotte, NC. Our goal is to outperform the S&P 500 by 5 10% per year, after fees. letters.lead_summary: The first quarter of 2026 was a disaster for our fund  due to the SaaSpocalypse, which rivalled the March 2020 pandemic in severity  despite very healthy KPIs across our holdings.  Our companies are diversified across many end markets, but as AI fears drove software correlations toward 1.0, our aggressive stance (long 130-145% of NAV) made our drawdown severe despite our high-quality portfolio. letters.lead_summary: Analog Century generated sizable alpha in 1Q 2026. Our long/short fund returned +14.1% net in the quarter, while the NASDAQ fell 7.0% and the S&P 500 declined 4.3%. Long/short net exposure averaged +50.2% and gross exposure averaged 114.2% in the quarter. Since its inception in August 2018, our long/short strategy has annualized net returns of +20.4% and has returned +314.6% net of fees vs. +162.4% for the S&P 500 Total Return Index (SPTR) and +199.8% for the NASDAQ TR. letters.lead_summary: The world has changed in three strange months, but BVF’s strategy has not.  I continue to invest your capital with eyes glued to the horizon.  Our portfolio is full of good, financially sound companies, led by capable people, bought at attractive prices.  In January, I told you that prudently sticking to these basics is the best antidote I can offer, amidst elevated uncertainty and a pricey stock market.  We now see uncertainty spiking further.  My tactics remain the same: bu

letters.lead_summary: Managing Member – Tim Eriksen     Eriksen Capital Management, LLC     8695 Glendale Road, Custer, WA 98240 The three major US indices we compare against were down in the first quarter primarily due to increased uncertainty in the Middle East.  In the first quarter, the NASDAQ declined 7.1%, the S&P500 (SPY) fell 4.4%, and the DJIA fell 3.3% .  Other indices we track were slightly positive in the quarter. Cedar Creek was up 6.8% on the quarter, net of fees and expenses.1

letters.lead_summary: This letter marks the beginning of Crestbreak Capital and outlines the firm’s background, investment philosophy, and capital allocation framework. letters.lead_summary: The first quarter was an eventful period for equity markets and geopolitical conditions. Market participants grappled with three key “what if” scenarios during the quarter, and the resulting uncertainty weighed on overall stock market performance. letters.lead_summary: We realize that the size of KEDM may be intimidating—it shouldn’t be. We have broken it down in 3 chapters: Thematics, Kliff Notes, and Event-Driven KEDM works whether you’ve got 2 minutes or a weekend. We hope you find it as valuable as we do! Our weekly deep dive into a big picture theme or macro event. Here, we flag what we consider to be the most interesting events of the week. And finally, +25 monitors with direct links to sources: press releases, SEC filings, and invest

We have broken it down in 3 chapters: Thematics, Kliff Notes, and Event-Driven KEDM works whether you’ve got 2 minutes or a weekend. We hope you find it as valuable as we do! Our weekly deep dive into a big picture theme or macro event. Here, we flag what we consider to be the most interesting events of the week.

Here, we flag what we consider to be the most interesting events And finally, +25 monitors with direct links to sources: press releases, SEC filings, and investor decks. We realize that the size of KEDM may be intimidating—it shouldn’t be. We have broken it down in 3 chapters: Thematics, Kliff Notes, and Event-Driven Monitors. KEDM works whether you’ve got 2 minutes or a weekend

small number of stocks represent such a large share of the index, conversations about what is
“wrong” with Nvidia, Amazon, Broadcom (AVGO), or Microsoft have become almost
indistinguishable from conversations about what is “wrong” with the market itself. Oil, credit, and
geopolitical stress have all had large moves and have catalyzed bouts of broad-based de-risking.

letters.lead_summary: The Norbury Capital Fund was down -3.6% in March outperforming the MSCI Europe Small-Cap index which was down -8.7%. Year-to-date the fund is down -2.0% (versus -3.3% for the index). Global markets had a rough month as a consequence of the war in Iran and the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz. letters.lead_summary: uring my 31 years at Parnassus, I’ve experienced several periods when markets and humanity were tested at the same time—from the tragedy of 9/11 to the Iraq War in 2003 to the Global Financial Crisis and the uncertainty of the COVID-19 pandemic. I still remember coming up the elevators to our office after 9/11 when markets reopened following four days of closure. It was a moment that underscored both the gravity of the world around us and the responsibility we carry as invest

letters.lead_summary: The Pernas Portfolio was down 6.4% in the first quarter vs. a 4.3% decline in the S&P 500. The year started strong, but we sold off sharply at the onset of the US/Iran war as commodity prices surged and inflation fears took center stage. We love risk but, like many investors, hate uncertainty. With risk, there is an ROI that derives from the time and effort spent on the analytical process. letters.lead_summary: The benefit is not that AI makes investment decisions easier. Rather, it allows more time to focus on the highest-value activities — understanding businesses deeply, refining investment decisions, and communicating with investors. The core philosophy of Right Tail remains unchanged. What is evolving are the tools that help support the work. In many ways, the role of technology in investing is similar to what we see in places like Kiawah. New tools can enhance the experience,

The strategy is generally 100% -16 0% long and 50% -75% short with a net exposure of 55%- 85%. letters.lead_summary: Please forgive the late letter. March passed with little to report from the portfolio, and it was just too tempting to see whether April might bring news. In the event, May was the month. On May 20 the fund brought suit in Harris County, TX against Bank of New York Mellon, the trustee for Pacific Coast Oil Trust, alleging breach of contract, breach of fiduciary duty, gross negligence, willful misconduct, and negligent misrepresentation.

letters.lead_summary: STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL – NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION Dear Investors and Friends, Sunriver’s Hedge Fund1 returned (11.6%) in Q1 2026. On an inception-to-date basis3, the Hedge Fund is compounding net returns at 6.9%. Sunriver’s Long-Only Fund1 returned (14.9%) in Q1 2026. On an inception-to-date basis3, the Long -Only Fund is compounding net returns at 5.4%.

The other day, an Oaktree colleague asked me about the developments that brought the credit sector to where it is today.
